title: RISK: Broadcast subscriber check is cached at connect time, never re-evaluated
description: relay/src/ws.rs lines 139-140: is_subscribed is cached from subscription_expiry() at auth time and never re-checked. If the subscription expires while the client is connected, the relay will silently let the client continue sending broadcasts (because require_subscription flag is checked against the stale is_subscribed bool, not a fresh DB lookup). When the relay server is restarted or the client reconnects, access is suddenly denied. Phase 1 behavior acceptable (no payment wiring yet), but will cause confusing access violations in Phase 3. A solo maintainer will see 'subscription expired 30 minutes ago but client still sends?' and not immediately understand the caching issue. Risk: subscription expirations silently violated until reconnect; confusing behavior in Phase 3.
close_reason: By design. Code already has comment: 'Cached at connect time. Re-evaluated only on reconnect, which is fine for Phase 1.' This is an accepted Phase 1 limitation, not a bug.
